Title: "Residual Mercury Content in Dental Amalgam Restorations"
Summary: This study analyzed various amalgam samples and found that maintaining a higher residual mercury content within the range of 58-62% significantly improves the mechanical properties such as fracture toughness and fatigue life compared to lower contents below this threshold.
